meaning: n.鏁ｅ竷,鍒嗘暎,娑堟暎,椹辨暎,鐤忔暎
examples: I can imagine that seed dispersal might be a factor.
Plant species dependent solely on seeds for survival or dispersal are obviously more vulnerable to any decrease in plant fecundity that occurs.
The structure of cycads male cones is quite consistent with the wind dispersal of pollen, clouds of which are released from some of the larger cones.
The troops responded with tear gas and other riot dispersal means, the military said.
And all this is fueling a dispersal of business school talent around the globe.
